title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: I'm going to teach you natural English that you can use when you travel. To kick things off, I came to eat one of Busan's most famous foods, Doiju Gukbap. Actually, I lied. I got Doiju Udon.
Let's give it a shot. Smells amazing. I'm going to try the broth first.

Just how I remembered. Crispy on the outside, sweet and nutty on the inside. So yummy. I'm going to be on a sugar high after this though. Are you going to eat them both? No. These are mine. Didn't you order one? Gritty. I'll let you have one bite if you're nice. How about that? I have a big one bite.
I love jjormyeon. Manjoo is good too. I'm not going to eat it. I'm not going to eat it.

title: will / shall
examples: There are tons of vendors. I love the energy. Today, I just took a look around the market, but I'm definitely coming back to enjoy some of the delicious seafood next time. Right now, I'm trying to save my appetite for our next stop. You'll see why. Our next stop is within walking distance. It only takes about five minutes to get there on foot.

Just how I remembered. Crispy on the outside, sweet and nutty on the inside. So yummy. I'm going to be on a sugar high after this though. Are you going to eat them both? No. These are mine. Didn't you order one? Gritty. I'll let you have one bite if you're nice. How about that? I have a big one bite.

Sunset time. The best time of day. I really wanted to ride in the capsules, but the tickets were completely sold out. Apparently, you have to book weeks in advance. So I'm hopping on the beach train instead. It runs along the same route, and they take walk-ups. We'll be departing shortly. Yellow, my favorite color. Look at that. Yellow, number one.

Personally, I just love walking on the beach and taking it all in. Today was like a mini staycation for me. It was so much fun. Check out the pins, comment below, and I will see you next time. Goodbye!

title: can / could / be able to
examples: I'm going to teach you natural English that you can use when you travel. To kick things off, I came to eat one of Busan's most famous foods, Doiju Gukbap. Actually, I lied. I got Doiju Udon.

It's the perfect keepsake. BTS, my grandma would be sad she's not here right now. My grandma is army. It is my bias. I'm army. There are so many cute souvenir shops selling little trinkets here, but I already got my souvenir. Gamcheon is so colorful and charming, but it's time to move on to my next destination. Jaregachi Market is one of the most famous seafood markets in Korea. It's the perfect place for seafood lovers. And since Busan is a coastal city, everything is especially fresh here. I can't believe the size of some of the fish here. They're huge. It's so lively here.
True. You could probably eat this whole thing in one bite. I can never get sick of this taste.

Never. This area is always super packed, and it kind of reminds me of Myeongdong because there are so many different food stands here. They have tteokbokki, they have fish cake, everything you could ever want, so make sure to come here hungry. I actually attended the opening ceremony of Biff Bissier.

The train ride was only about 10 minutes long, and maybe this is an obvious piece of advice, but make sure you come in the daytime. We couldn't see anything, but in my defense, we came at the perfect time of day, but there was no availability, and we had to wait, and it got dark.

title: Passive (is done / was done)
examples: So, it's a bit different from what I've had in the past. This place is called Hapchan Iryu, Doiju Gukbap. And the line outside was crazy. I definitely think it's very popular with the locals.
